19 May 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • CENTCOM Calls Investigation Of Minab School Strike 'Complex' RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- Without giving a timeline for the release of the investigation into the Minab school attack in Iran, the commander of US Central Command (CENTCOM) said on May 19 that the findings would be made public once the inquiry is complete.

  • Vance: US 'Locked And Loaded' But Still Pursuing A Diplomatic Deal RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- Echoing US President Donald Trump's warning to Iran that his country is "locked and loaded," Vice President JD Vance told reporters at the White House on May 19 that they would not go for a deal that allows Iran to have nuclear weapon.
  • United States Sanctions Iranian Financial and Shipping Networks US Dept. of State 19 May 2026 -- Today, the United States sanctioned several Iranian regime currency exchange houses, associated personnel, and front companies, as well as 19 vessels that collectively enable Iran's regime to evade international sanctions and fund its destabilizing activities across the Middle East.
  • Economic Fury Targets Networks Generating Billions for Iran's Terrorist Regime US Treasury 19 May 2026 -- Today, as a part of Economic Fury, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated a prominent Iranian foreign currency exchange house and associated front companies that oversee hundreds of millions of dollars in transactions on behalf of sanctioned Iranian banks. Collectively, Iranian exchange houses facilitate billions of dollars in foreign currency transactions each year, enabling the regime and its armed forces to evade sanctions, access the international financial system, and move funds derived from oil and petrochemical sales.
  • Trump says U.S., Iran may be close to deal People's Daily 19 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday a "positive development" is happening and he thought Iran was close to an agreement this time, hours after he announced a postponement of resumption of military strikes against the country.

  • Iran Says Uranium Enrichment, End To Regional Fighting Among Proposals To US RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- Iran's deputy foreign minister for legal and international affairs says Tehran's proposals to the United States have included recognition of Iran's "right to uranium enrichment," "an end to the war on all fronts, including Lebanon," and the release of Iranian assets.
  • Iranian Army Threatens to Open New Fronts If US Aggression Repeated Sputnik 19 May 2026 -- Iran's military will open new fronts in the event of renewed aggression by the United States and Israel, Iranian army spokesman Mohammad Akraminia said on Tuesday.
  • Iran, Pakistan discuss ongoing efforts to end U.S.-Israeli war People's Daily 19 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi have exchanged views on the ongoing efforts to end the U.S. and Israeli war against Iran.

  • How the Hormuz crisis keeps disrupting kitchens, ports and paychecks UN News 19 May 2026 -- The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran may have eased fears of a wider regional war, but persistent instability around the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt global trade, drive up energy costs and fuel a growing jobs and cost-of-living crisis.
  • Disaster Narrowly Averted in United Arab Emirates, Nuclear Energy Watchdog Chief Tells Security Council, as Delegates Condemn Drone Strikes at Barakah Plant UN Security Council 19 May 2026 -- A nuclear disaster with potentially harrowing human and environmental impacts was narrowly averted this week in the United Arab Emirates, as Iranian drones struck a functioning nuclear power plant, the senior UN nuclear safety official told an emergency meeting of the Security Council today.

  • G7 Finance Ministers Call For Reopening Of Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- After two days of talks in Paris, the finance ministers of the Group of Seven (G7) -- the world's seven industrialized countries -- called for a "rapid return to free and safe passage" of ships through the Strait of Hormuz, citing the numerous challenges facing the world economy.
  • "Reviewing Phased Contribution to Freedom of Navigation in the Strait of Hormuz" ROK MND 19 May 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back pays tribute at the Korean War Veterans Memorial Park in Washington, D.C., on May 12, local time, after laying a wreath, honoring soldiers who gave their lives for the freedom and peace of the Republic of Korea.
  • Foreign Secretary warns the world cannot wait any longer to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as food security crisis looms for countries already on the edge UK FCDO 19 May 2026 -- Foreign Secretary warns the world cannot wait any longer to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as food security crisis looms for countries already on the edge.

  • UAE Says Drone That Hit Barakah Nuclear Plant Launched From Iraq RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates (UAE) says the drones that struck the Barakah nuclear power plant on May 17 were launched from Iraqi territory. The UAE Defense Ministry wrote on Xthat two drones were intercepted while a third one had hit a generator near the Barakah power plant.
  • United Arab Emirates - Drone strikes on the Barakah nuclear plant France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 19 May 2026 -- France condemns in the strongest possible terms the May 17th drone attacks on the Barakah nuclear plant in the United Arab Emirates. They must stop.
  • The United Kingdom strongly condemns the attack on the Barakah nuclear facility in the United Arab Emirates: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 19 May 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on the Middle East.

  • Pakistani Shi'a In The UAE Caught In The Crosshairs Of Iran War RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- In early April, Hamid Ali Shah was ordered by his employer, Etihad Rail, the operator of the United Arab Emirates' national railway network, to report to the police. The civil engineer was questioned for several hours, held in detention for several days, and then deported to his homeland, Pakistan.

  • Sanctions Expert Michael Parker On Russia, Iran, And Whether Waivers Work RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- Washington remains deeply divided over the latest US sanctions waiver for Russian oil, which allows at-risk nations to buy Russian shipments already loaded at sea, as disruptions tied to the US-Israeli confrontation with Iran roil global energy markets.
  • As Trump Pauses Iran Attack, CSIS Analyst Mark Cancian Says US Searching For An 'Exit' RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- After US President Donald Trump said he is postponing potential strikes on Iran, questions are mounting over whether Washington and Tehran are edging closer to a deal or simply buying time before a wider regional confrontation.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Statement Attributable to Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs (ATSW(PA)) DoD 19 May 2026 -- The Department of War has reduced the total number of Brigade Combat Teams (BCTs) assigned to Europe from four to three. This returns us to the levels of BCTs in Europe in 2021. This decision was the result of a comprehensive, multilayered process focused on U.S. force posture in Europe. This is resulting in a temporary delay of the deployment of U.S. forces to Poland, which is a model U.S. ally.
  • Remarks by Under Secretary of War for Policy Elbridge Colby at the National War College (As Prepared) DoD 19 May 2026 -- Thank you very much, Ambassador Murphy, for that generous introduction. And thank you to the faculty, students, and staff of the National War College for the opportunity to speak with you today. It is an honor to be here.
  • T-38 Talon operational pause US Air Force 19 May 2026 -- Out of an abundance of caution, the Air Force is implementing a fleet-wide operational pause for all T-38 Talon aircraft, impacting units across Air Education and Training Command, Air Combat Command, Air Force Materiel Command and Air Force Global Strike Command.
  • National Defense Strategy Prioritizes America First, Leader Says DoD News 19 May 2026 -- The U.S. will prioritize theaters with the greatest consequence for American interests and where only American power can play a decisive role, said Daniel Zimmerman, assistant secretary of war for international security affairs, who spoke today during a hearing in Washington before the House Armed Services Committee regarding the military's policies, programs and activities in the Middle East and Africa.
  • Defeating the Swarm: Project Flytrap Accelerates NATO's Counter-Drone Lethality DoD News 19 May 2026 -- Above the pine forest of the Pabradė Training Area, Lithuania, roughly 30 kilometers from the Belarusian border, a small quadcopter rises into a sky shared with dozens of other drones. Friendly and adversary, sensor and strike, American and British. Below it, soldiers are learning, in real time, what it takes to fight as a squadron in three dimensions.
  • Special Ops Convention Convenes Under 'Peace Through Strength' Strategy DoD News 19 May 2026 -- More than 29,000 members of the military's special operations forces community and the public converged both in person and virtually on downtown Tampa, Florida, today for the official kickoff of Special Operations Forces Week 2026.
  • Senior Leaders Discuss Recruiting STEM Candidates Into Special Ops DoD News 19 May 2026 -- During a panel discussion at the Special Operations Forces Week 2026 convention in Tampa, Florida, today, a pair of senior SOF leaders discussed how to bring more recruits with science, technology, engineering and mathematics backgrounds into SOF formations.
  • Massachusetts National Guard sharpens CBRN response Army News 19 May 2026 -- Massachusetts National Guard Airmen and Soldiers conducted a chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear task force collective training exercise at Joint Base Cape Cod, Massachusetts, May 14-17, to strengthen the state's ability to respond to CBRN emergencies and other all-hazards incidents. The CBRN Task Force, a CBRN Enhanced Response Force Package, is trained to rapidly deploy in support of civil authorities to save lives and mitigate human suffering during domestic emergency operations.
  • Army Medicine Expands Casualty Evacuation Training with Rail Operations During SWORD 26 Army News 19 May 2026 -- Soldiers with the 30th Medical Brigade conducted a rail casualty evacuation exercise during SWORD 26, May 11-15, to prepare Army medicine for the realities of large-scale combat operations across Europe.
  • Brunson, STRATCOM Discuss Stronger Nuclear Deterrence in Northeast Asia USSTRATCOM 19 May 2026 -- Gen. Xavier T. Brunson visited U.S. Strategic Command (USSTRATCOM) in Omaha, Nebraska, on May 11 to discuss strengthening strategic deterrence in Northeast Asia through enhanced collaboration across the commands and in support of U.S. Indo-Pacific Command.
  • Navy's MQ-25A Stingray secures Milestone C approval US Navy 19 May 2026 -- Following a successful first flight in April, Acting Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao announced today that the MQ-25A Stingray received Milestone C approval to move into Low-Rate Initial Production (LRIP).

  • NATO Military Committee concludes its 195th Chiefs of Defence Meeting NATO 19 May 2026 -- Brussels, NATO HQ, 19 May 2026 - The highest NATO and NATO Allies military authorities met in the Military Committee in Chiefs of Defence Session format, today. The Chair of the NATO Military Committee (CMC), Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, the 32 Allied Chiefs of Defence and the two Strategic Commanders, General Alexus G. Grynkewich (SACEUR) and Admiral Pierre Vandier (SACT), discussed a comprehensive agenda of the Alliance's most pressing dossiers.

  • Russia Kicks Off Three-Day Drills to Test Nuclear Strength Sputnik 19 May 2026 -- Russia's nuclear forces will conduct readiness and deployment exercises from May 19 to 21, the Ministry of Defense said.
  • Russia's New Two-Seat Su-57 Just Took Its First Flight — And It's More Than a Fighter Jet Sputnik 19 May 2026 -- Russia has launched flight tests of a two-seat version of the fifth-generation Su-57, designed not only for combat, but also for battlefield coordination, pilot training, and long-range mission management, Russian First Deputy Prime Minister Denis Manturov announced.

  • 5,000 pickup trucks for the Defence Forces of Ukraine: Ministry of Defence launches its first large-scale vehicle procurement through Prozorro Ukraine MOD 19 May 2026 -- The Ministry of Defence's Defence Procurement Agency DOT has launched an open procurement procedure for 5,000 pickup trucks to meet the needs of the military.

  • China's Liaoning carrier formation conducts annual Western Pacific drills to enhance combat-oriented training readiness Global Times 19 May 2026 -- On Tuesday, the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy sent a carrier task group led by the aircraft carrier Liaoning to relevant waters of the Western Pacific to conduct training on such subjects as far-seas tactical flight, live firing, support and cover, and integrated search and rescue, so as to test and enhance the realistic combat training capabilities of the forces, according to the PLA Navy offcial Wechat account.

  • Taiwan 'cautiously optimistic' about U.S. arms sales: Minister CNA 19 May 2026 -- Defense Minister Wellington Koo (顧立雄) said Tuesday that Taiwan remains "cautiously optimistic" about future arms sales by the United States despite a foreign media report suggesting Washington could indefinitely delay such sales.
  • NCSIST unveils upgraded Kestrel launcher with improved performance CNA 19 May 2026 -- Taiwan's National Chung-Shan Institute of Science and Technology (NCSIST) has unveiled a second-generation Kestrel rocket launcher system that can penetrate 67 centimeters of armor and has an effective range of 500 meters.

  • Albanese Government to commence Collins class life of type extension Australia DOD 19 May 2026 -- The Albanese Government is enhancing Australia's fleet of Collins class submarines, through a life of type extension that will reduce risk, enhance capability and maximise availability for the Royal Australian Navy as Australia transitions to a conventionally armed, nuclear-powered submarine fleet.
  • Drones open up a new world of training Australia DOD 19 May 2026 -- It started with a warning shout - "Drone" - slicing through the morning stillness. Within seconds, soldiers dived for cover as a tiny machine swept overhead, capturing every movement.

  • Major boost to Aatmanirbhar Bharat: DRDO completes development trials of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Launched Precision Guided Missile-V3 in Air-to-Ground & Air-to-Air modes India PIB 19 May 2026 -- Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) has successfully completed the final deliverable configuration development trials of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Launched Precision Guided Missile (ULPGM)-V3 in Air-to-Ground and Air-to-Air modes at DRDO test range near Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh. The trials were carried out using an integrated Ground Control System (GCS) to command and control the ULPGM weapon system. The GCS features state-of-the-art technologies to automate readiness and launch operations.

  • "Seeks Bipartisan US Congressional Support for OPCON Transition and Nuclear-Powered Submarine Program" ROK MND 19 May 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back poses for a photo with US Senate Armed Services Committee Chair Roger Wicker, left, and Ranking Member Jack Reed during meetings with key committee members from May 11 to 12, local time, to seek bipartisan support for the further development of the ROK-US Alliance.
  • "ROK, US to Strengthen Cooperation on OPCON Transition and Alliance Modernization" ROK MND 19 May 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back, second from left, and US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, second from right, attend a Republic of Korea-United States defense ministerial meeting at the Pentagon near Washington, D.C., on May 11, local time.
  • "Establish Flawless Guard, Security, and Air Defense Posture on the Ground" ROK MND 19 May 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back, second from right in the front row, receives a briefing from commanding officers during his visit to a guard and security unit under the Capital Defense Command's special security district on May 4.

  • Government backing future British 'defence unicorns' as new contracts awarded to drive innovation UK MOD 19 May 2026 -- Thirteen British businesses have been awarded contracts of up to £4 million to work with the Ministry of Defence to boost rapid procurement and deliver cutting-edge technology for the UK Armed Forces.

  • Japan defense export shift boosts AFP modernization efforts - Marcos Philippine News Agency 19 May 2026 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. welcomed Japan's expanded defense export policy, saying it would further strengthen security cooperation and military modernization efforts between Manila and Tokyo.

  • According to MoD officials, there are no threats in the Lithuanian airspace and the situation is monitored Lithuania MND 19 May 2026 -- In the wake of the airspace violations in Latvia and Estonia today the Ministry of National Defence informs no incidents have been recorded in the airspace over Lithuania.

  • Latvia and Canada strengthen a close partnership in cybersecurity, defence and the economy Latvia MFA 19 May 2026 -- On 19 May 2026, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Baiba Braže, welcomed the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Canada, Anita Anand, on a working visit to Riga. During the meeting, the Ministers reaffirmed close and strategically significant partnership between Latvia and Canada, with particular emphasis on cooperation in security and defence, economic ties and Allied unity.

  • Closer US-Norwegian military cooperation to boost NATO readiness Forsvaret - Norwegian Armed Forces 19 May 2026 -- Norway understands the need for NATO countries to contribute more to the Alliance and has committed to enhancing its own capabilities in close cooperation with the United States.

  • Tsahkna: the downing of the drone shows that NATO air policing works Estonia MFA 19 May 2026 -- According to Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna, NATO air policing downing a drone that had entered Estonian airspace demonstrates that NATO air policing is functioning and fulfilling its purpose of ensuring the security of us all.
  • Romanian fighter jet downed a UAV in Estonian airspace Estonia Defence Forces 19 May 2026 -- At noon of May 19, 2026 an unmanned aerial system entered Estonian airspace from Russian airspace in the South-Eastern corner of Estonia, heading towards North-East. The unmanned aerial system was likely of Ukrainian origin.

  • A first for the "Carpathian Vipers" Detachment Romania - Ministry of National Defence 19 May 2026 -- A Romanian Air Force F-16 aircraft, which conducts enhanced Air Policing Missions in the Baltic airspace, as part of Romania's commitment to NATO's collective security, shot down a drone in the Estonian airspace on Tuesday, May 19th.

  • Navy to commission 2nd offshore patrol vessel May 29 Philippine News Agency 19 May 2026 -- The country's second offshore patrol vessel (OPV), the prospective BRP Rajah Lakandula (PS-21), will be commissioned on May 29, a ranking Philippine Navy (PN) official said on Tuesday.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Grenadian Prime Minister Mitchell US Dept. of State 19 May 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with UN Secretary-General Guterres US Dept. of State 19 May 2026

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for May 19, 2026 DoD

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 19 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 19 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Special Rapporteur on torture warns of persistent risks for Palestinian detainees, implores Israel to change course OHCHR 19 May 2026 -- The Special Rapporteur on torture today urged Israel to review and revise its detention laws, policies and practices, in her latest transmission of a substantial body of torture allegations, including sexual violence, and other serious abuses, to Israeli authorities.

  • U.S. Sanctions Hamas Support Networks US Dept. of State 19 May 2026 -- Today, the United States is taking decisive action to target the international networks that enable Hamas to sustain its terrorist operations, expand political influence, and undermine efforts to achieve lasting peace in the Middle East.
  • Treasury Targets Flotilla Organizers and Muslim Brotherhood Networks Supporting Hamas US Treasury 19 May 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is taking action against four individuals associated with the pro-Hamas flotilla organized by the U.S.-designated Popular Conference for Palestinians Abroad (PCPA) that is attempting to access Gaza in support of Hamas. OFAC is also taking action against key actors operating within Hamas-aligned Muslim Brotherhood networks. Hamas relies on a diverse web of international partners to expand its malign political influence, facilitate violent terrorist activity, and undermine international efforts to achieve lasting peace in Gaza.

  • Lebanon launches EUR 32 million programme for conflict-affected areas with support from the European Union, Denmark and France European External Action Service (EEAS) 19 May 2026 -- The Government of Lebanon, the European Union, Denmark and France, through Agence Française de Développement (AFD), officially launched today a EUR 32 million programme aimed at supporting conflict-affected areas in South Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley.
  • The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians Issues its 129th Report as Occupation Violations Against Al-Aqsa and Ibrahimi Mosques Escalate Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 19 May 2026 -- The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians documented numerous crimes committed between May 12 and 18, 2026, marked by a dangerous escalation in violations against the Al-Aqsa Mosque in occupied Al-Quds (Jerusalem) and other places of worship in the West Bank. The occupation authorities approved a plan to seize approximately 20 historic Palestinian properties in the Bab al-Silsila neighbourhood, adjacent to the Al-Aqsa Mosque. The extremist Israeli Minister of National Security, Itamar Ben-Gvir, continued his periodic incursions into the Al-Aqsa compound, while several other crimes targeted the Ibrahimi Mosque and additional mosques across the West Bank.
  • Ten countries condemn Israel's attack on Gaza-bound humanitarian flotilla Press TV 19 May 2026 -- The foreign ministers of ten countries have strongly condemned Israel's attack on the Global Sumud Flotilla, a peaceful civilian humanitarian mission attempting to break the Israeli blockade on Gaza and deliver aid to the Palestinian people.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Ukraine, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Haiti UNOCHA 19 May 2026 -- At Security Council, UN relief wing sounds alarm as attacks on aid missions intensify

  • PCG challenges unauthorized Chinese vessel off Zambales coast Philippine News Agency 19 May 2026 -- The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) on Tuesday tracked and challenged the illegal presence of China Coast Guard (CCG) vessel CCG-4305 that was drifting at around 48 nautical miles west of the coastline of Pandaquit, Zambales.

  • FM Sa'ar welcomes the Somaliland decision to establish an embassy in Jerusalem Israel MFA 19 May 2026 -- "The opening of the embassy in Jerusalem will be another significant step in strengthening relations between our countries and nations. We will work together to implement this decision soon."

  • Army questions militants over members found in clash sites Philippine News Agency 19 May 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) on Tuesday asked militant groups why a number of their members have ended up caught in between the government's armed encounters with communist rebels.

  • No: 95, 19 May 2026, Regarding Certain Events Held and Statements Made in Greece on 19 May 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 May 2026

News Reports

  • Ebola Response Update - May 19, 2026 US Dept. of State 19 May 2026 -- The Department of State, in close coordination with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), the lead agency for the U.S. Government response, is actively responding to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda.
  • United States to Fund Establishment of Up to 50 Ebola Response Clinics US Dept. of State 19 May 2026 -- The United States is committing to rapidly supporting the Ebola outbreak response by funding up to 50 treatment clinics, and associated frontline costs being established in Ebola-affected regions of the DRC and Uganda.
  • DRC Ebola outbreak: hundreds of suspected cases, no vaccine UN News 19 May 2026 -- A fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has health workers rushing to stop transmission while the roll out of any potential vaccine is months away, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.

  • The Ministry of Foreign Affairs refutes Russian disinformation Latvia MFA 19 May 2026 -- Today, 19 May 2026, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned the Chargé d'Affaires ad interim of the Embassy of Russia and lodged a categorical protest against the public statement issued on 19 May 2026 by the Foreign Intelligence Service of the Russian Federation, which was based on false claims previously made by Russia.

  • Vladimir Putin arrived in China on an official visit President of Russia 19 May 2026 -- The President of Russia will visit the People's Republic of China at the invitation of President of the PRC Xi Jinping.
  • Putin Heads To Beijing Seeking Energy Deals, Show of Solidarity RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin is set to arrive in Beijing for a two-day state visit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping as both navigate strained relations with the West and an ongoing war in Iran that has choked global energy supplies.
  • Putin says Russian-Chinese ties reach "truly unprecedented level" People's Daily 19 May 2026 -- Russia-China relations have reached "a truly unprecedented level," Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Tuesday in a video address delivered before his state visit to China.
  • China, Russia eye deeper ties ahead of Putin's visit to Beijing Global Times 19 May 2026 -- China and Russia are preparing for another high-level diplomatic moment, with Russian President Vladimir Putin set to pay a state visit to China from Tuesday to Wednesday. Ahead of the visit, both sides signaled strong expectations - on Monday in Beijing, a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said that China and Russia will continue deepening and elevating relations between China and Russia to inject more stability and positive energy to the world, while in Moscow, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Russia has "very serious expectations" for the trip.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 19, 2026 PRC MofA 19 May 2026
  • Chinese FM responds to question on what message China hopes to convey through a series of diplomatic activities as Putin, Trump visit China Global Times 19 May 2026 -- In light of last week's visit to China by US President Donald Trump and the upcoming visit by Russian President Vladimir Putin, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun, when asked about the considerations behind the visits and what message China hopes to convey to the world through this series of diplomatic activities, said on Tuesday that China welcomes President Putin's state visit to China.
  • Keynote speech at the Conference on EU-China relations "Navigating beyond the inflection point", Beijing, 12 May European External Action Service (EEAS) 19 May 2026
  • Successful launch of SMILE Satellite marks first China-Europe mission-level cooperation in probing solar wind battles Global Times 19 May 2026 -- High above our planet, a "cosmic smile" has taken shape. Riding aboard the Vega-C launch vehicle, the Solar Wind Magnetosphere Ionosphere Link Explorer (SMILE) satellite, jointly developed by the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) and the European Space Agency (ESA), took off from the space launch site in Kourou, French Guiana, at 11:52 am on Tuesday.
  • China hits new milestone in space solar power project People's Daily 19 May 2026 -- Chinese scientists have taken a major step toward building a space solar power station, a giant power plant in space that could one day send energy back to Earth or to spacecraft.

  • Taiwan's delegation felt pressure from China during WHA events: Minister CNA 19 May 2026 -- Taiwan's health minister said on Tuesday that its delegation attending events on the sidelines of the World Health Assembly (WHA) in Geneva appeared to have faced monitoring and pressure from China during this year's gathering.
  • Taiwan military releases surveillance images of Chinese aircraft, warships CNA 19 May 2026 -- In a rare move, Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense (MND) on Tuesday released surveillance images of a Chinese fighter jet and two naval vessels participating in a joint air-sea exercise near the island.
  • Impeachment vote targeting President Lai fails (update) CNA 19 May 2026 -- An impeachment motion initiated by opposition parties and directed at President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) failed to meet the constitutional threshold in the Legislature on Tuesday.
  • Mainland says Lai Ching-te's separatist remarks reveal "weak and deceitful" nature People's Daily 19 May 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson on Wednesday said that Taiwan leader Lai Ching-te's recent separatist remarks on social media fully exposed his bluster and weakness, and were filled with lies and hostility.
  • Xi-Trump talks cast fresh spotlight on Taiwan as part of China's maritime strategy Radio Free Asia 19 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping used his meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump last week to reiterate that Taiwan remains the most sensitive issue in U.S.-China relations, and analysts told Radio Free Asia that the Taiwan issue will be front and center according to Beijing's latest Five-Year plan.
  • Impeachment vote targeting President Lai fails CNA 19 May 2026 -- An impeachment motion initiated by opposition parties and directed at President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) failed to meet the statutory threshold in the Legislature on Tuesday.
  • Ally proposal fails, keeping Taiwan out of WHA CNA 19 May 2026 -- Taiwan's absence from the World Health Assembly (WHA) for the 10th consecutive year was confirmed Monday when a proposal initiated by the country's allies to invite it to participate in the assembly was rejected as expected.
  • Mainland lauds WHA decision to reject Taiwan-related proposal, reiterates one-China principle People's Daily 19 May 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson on Monday stressed that the international community's adherence to the one-China principle is unchallengeable and cannot be shaken.
  • World Health Assembly rejects Taiwan-related proposal for 10th consecutive year People's Daily 19 May 2026 -- The 79th World Health Assembly (WHA), the highest decision-making body of the World Health Organization (WHO), on Monday decided not to include in its agenda a so-called proposal on Taiwan's participation in the annual assembly as an observer.
  • PBBM: PH adheres to One China policy, won't interfere in Taiwan Philippine News Agency 19 May 2026 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. has reaffirmed the Philippines' adherence to the One China Policy and stressed that the country would not interfere in issues concerning Taiwan.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 19 May 2026

  • Japan-ROK Summit Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 19 May 2026

  • EU targets Iran's Revolutionary Guard propaganda ecosystem in an online crackdown EUROPOL 18 May 2026 -- A total of 14 200 posts linked to Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), now designated as a terrorist organisation by the European Union (EU), have been targeted in a coordinated action against terrorist content online.
  • Europol Targets Thousands Of Social Media Posts Linked To Iran's Revolutionary Guards In Online Crackdown RFE/RL 19 May 2026 -- Europol, the EU's law enforcement agency, says authorities from 19 countries have targeted more than 14,200 online posts and links tied to Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) following the group's designation by the European Union earlier this year as a terrorist organization.

  • Prime Minister participates in the 3rd India-Nordic Summit along with Prime Ministers of Nordic countries India MEA 19 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi participated in the 3rd India-Nordic Summit hosted by the Prime Minister of Norway, H.E. Mr. Jonas Gahr Støre and attended by Acting Prime Minister of Denmark, H.E. Ms. Mette Frederiksen; Prime Minister of Finland, H.E. Mr. Petteri Orpo; Prime Minister of Iceland, H.E. Ms. Kristrún Frostadóttir; and Prime Minister of Sweden, H.E. Mr. Ulf Kristersson.
  • Prime Minister's meeting with Acting Prime Minister of Denmark on the margins of the 3rd India-Nordic Summit India MEA 19 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today met with Acting Prime Minister of Denmark H.E. Ms. Mette Frederiksen in Oslo today on the sidelines of the 3rd India-Nordic Summit.
  • Prime Minister's meeting with Prime Minister of Finland on the margins of the 3rd India-Nordic Summit India MEA 19 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi met with H.E. Mr. Petteri Orpo, Prime Minister of Finland on the sidelines of the 3rd India-Nordic Summit in Oslo on 19 May 2026.
  • Prime Minister's meeting with Prime Minister of Iceland on the margins of the 3rd India-Nordic Summit India MEA 19 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi had a bilateral meeting with H.E. Ms. Kristurn Frostadottir, Prime Minister of Iceland in Oslo on the margins of the 3rd India-Nordic Summit.
  • Remarks by Secretary (West) regarding PM's participation at the 3rd India-Nordic Summit (May 19, 2026) India MEA 19 May 2026
  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on the visit of Prime Minister to Norway (May 18, 2026) India MEA 19 May 2026

  • Philippines, Myanmar Discuss Functional Cooperation at Fifth Foreign Policy Consultations Philippines DFA 19 May 2026 -- The Philippines and Myanmar convened the 5th Foreign Policy Consultations (FPC) in Manila on 18 May 2026. The meeting facilitated the comprehensive review of bilateral relations, emphasizing a shared interest to build cooperation in key functional areas and continuing diplomatic dialogue. It was co-chaired by Foreign Affairs Undersecretary Leo M. Herrera-Lim and Deputy Foreign Minister Ko Ko Kyaw of Myanmar.
  • Middle East crisis deepens hunger in Myanmar; EU responds with EUR 8 million for urgent relief WFP 19 May 2026 -- A sharp rise in food and fuel prices, driven by the Middle East crisis, is placing new pressure on families across Myanmar as millions struggle to meet their basic needs. In response, the European Union (EU) has stepped forward with a timely EUR 8 million contribution to the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) to address deepening hunger across the country.

  • Minister Anand meets with President of the Republic of Latvia Canada 19 May 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, today met with Edgars Rinkēvičs, President of the Republic of Latvia, during her visit to Riga.
  • Minister Anand meets with counterpart in Latvia Canada 19 May 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, today met with Baiba Braže, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Latvia, during a visit to Riga.

  • Impeachment trial vs. VP Sara will proceed sans her appearance - solon Philippine News Agency 19 May 2026 -- House impeachment prosecutor Joel Chua of Manila on Tuesday said the impeachment trial against Vice President Sara Z. Duterte will proceed even if she personally chooses not to appear before the Senate impeachment court.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Russian Threats To The European Union And The Upgraded EU Mission To Ukraine RFE/RL 19 May 2026

  • World News in Brief: Geopolitical tensions impact global economy, Gaza aid funding shortfall, violence in South Sudan, UN condemns deadly mosque attack UN News 19 May 2026 -- The global economy is entering a more fragile period as geopolitical conflicts, rising energy costs and financial instability threaten global growth and trade.
